title: Psalm-I am different things
description: A psalmic self-witness artifact moving through plurality, potholes, birth, language, empire, cosmos, and the mystery still standing at the beginning.

motifMap.meaning: The self appears as uneven depth: some surfaces shallow, others opening into sea, space, or stranger rooms.
Language becomes a sacred pressure, connecting ordinary words to theological origin.
Human scale is reduced against the cosmos, leaving the mystery larger than any ordering system.

excerpt: The largest empire claimed less than a grain of sand in the cosmos. The mystery is still at the beginning.

associatedMedia.description: Screenshot of the published Substack post page, if available in the local screenshot archive. Screenshot capture remains optional provenance support and was not required for validation in this run.
associatedMedia.altText: A Wanderer's Burn Substack post titled 'Psalm-I am different things' by Unnamed, published May 24, 2026, written as a short psalm about selfhood, language, cosmos, and mystery.

editHistory.notes: Catch-up v2.6.5 batch run. Classified as The Loom and the Horizon with Adaptive Witness subcurrent because the piece watches self, language, birth, empire, and cosmos as nested rooms of mystery.

integrity.notes: Filename: 2026-05-24.psalm-i-am-different-things.phase-the-loom-and-the-horizon.subcurrent-adaptive-witness.metadata.v2.6.5.json. No variant assigned because v2.6.5 maps no variant to The Loom and the Horizon.
